piece.title: Constellation Op 1
piece.notes: Faux-serial cinematic piece in B locrian. Four chromatic chords rotated across five movements. Transform sequence drives melodic mutation across the arc. Canon counterpoint in movement II. Total ~140 bars, approx 10-12 minutes at 96 BPM. transform_sequence corrected: original 4 entries wrapped across 6 sections, which silently misassigned two movements against their own stated intent -- suspension would have received retrograde_inversion instead of the retrograde its own notes describe, and inversion_mirror would have received original instead of the retrograde_inversion its own notes explicitly name. Extended to 6 entries, one per section. positions 0/1/3/4 (prime_statement/inversion_entry/suspension/inversion_mirror) are read directly from each section's own notes. positions 2/5 (contrapuntal_web/dissolution) have no single explicit transform claim in their notes and were a judgment call, not a correction -- worth confirming these two specifically.

sections.name: prime_statement
sections.notes: MOVEMENT I. Row introduced in original form. Sparse, angular, searching. The motif appears once or twice — no development yet, just declaration. Bass anchors on root only. No counterpoint. Arc holds flat — no emotional direction yet.

sections.name: inversion_entry
sections.notes: Row inversion enters. Melodic shape descends where it previously rose — the mirror image. Density increases slightly. Bass moves to melodic for forward motion. Still no counterpoint — the single voice is enough here.

sections.name: contrapuntal_web
sections.notes: MOVEMENT II. Full contrapuntal complexity. Canon at 1.5 bars offset — the row chasing itself. All four transforms active via transform_sequence. Walking bass drives forward relentlessly. Two breath waves across 32 bars. This is the emotional and intellectual center of the piece.

sections.name: suspension
sections.notes: MOVEMENT III. Slow movement — near silence after the complexity of Movement II. Sparse melody fragments, pedal bass, very long rests. The row appears in retrograde — backward, dissolving. No counterpoint. Arc holds flat. This is the void at the center of the piece.

sections.name: inversion_mirror
sections.notes: MOVEMENT IV. Mirror of Movement I but darker — retrograde_inversion now drives the melody. The same chromatic chords but entered from bVII, which shifts the harmonic gravity. Density builds back toward full. Bass becomes melodic again. Counterpoint re-enters below the melody — a reversal of Movement II where it sat above.

sections.name: dissolution
sections.notes: MOVEMENT V. The row breaks apart. Sparse, generative melody — fragments only, no complete statements. Bass pulls back to root_only. No counterpoint. Arc fades to nothing. The piece ends mid-thought, unresolved — serial logic demands no cadence.
